_comment: Judgment layer for scripts/generate-positioning.py — per-repo inbound positioning. The deepest ladder level is the recruiter bridge (see docs/inbound-magnet-system.md). internal_anchor values are NEVER published; they render only into the .internal artifact. PUBLIC pages carry NO prices (tool-enforced).

frontdoor._contact_note: Set `contact` to the inbound address you want on the public surface (a dedicated alias is wiser than a personal inbox — it gets indexed). When set, every CTA becomes a mailto: pre-tagged with the repo + door, so clicks land already-classified in your inbox triage. Empty = plain CTA text, nothing published. This is the one switch that turns the capture funnel live; it's your call which address.
frontdoor.headline: I build production systems that solve expensive problems.
frontdoor.subhead: Not demos. Live platforms — tested, deployed, and running. Each repo below is a working system someone can put to work today. Two doors:

door_client.label: Have a problem one of these solves? — Deploy it for your shop
door_client.blurb: Pick the depth that fits. We feed you the output, run it under your brand, build it for your exact world, or become your engine.

door_recruiter.label: Hiring someone who ships at this level? — Work with the builder
door_recruiter.blurb: Everything here is the portfolio. If you need a senior builder who owns systems end-to-end — data, infra, AI, deploy — this is the evidence.

frontdoor.closing: If any of this fits, reach out. This conversation starts at serious.

organvm/public-record-data-scrapper.what_it_is: A production platform that collects UCC-1 filings from all 50 Secretary-of-State portals, enriches each record with SEC / OSHA / USPTO / Census and key-gated premium sources, and scores every prospect 0–100 on financing likelihood with an A–F health grade — delivered via dashboard, REST API, and CLI.
organvm/public-record-data-scrapper.buyer: MCA funders, ISOs, and brokers who buy financing leads constantly.
organvm/public-record-data-scrapper.expensive_problem: Commodity aged UCC lists are cheap and worthless. Exclusive, fresh, enriched, scored UCC leads are the difference between a dialer full of dead numbers and a funded deal.
organvm/public-record-data-scrapper.cost_of_not_having_it: A single funded deal is worth a five-figure commission. Good data here pays for itself on the first conversion — and bad data burns your closers' hours on the dead numbers everyone else already called.
organvm/public-record-data-scrapper.public_face: the full platform — source, the 3,399 tests, the Terraform, all 60+ collection agents, the scoring method. Read every line.
organvm/public-record-data-scrapper.private_operation: the live, fed instance — fresh 50-state pulls, premium-keyed enrichment, scoring tuned to your underwriting, current this morning. You can read every line and still can't clone the running engine; that's the part you put to work.

ladder.name: Feed me leads
ladder.what_they_get: You never touch the platform. We deliver scored, enriched, exclusive UCC leads on a recurring feed — your states, your filters. Beats every commodity list you buy today.

ladder.name: Run it in my shop
ladder.what_they_get: The platform under your brand, on your infra (or our managed). You own the pipeline and stop renting lists forever.

ladder.name: Build it for my exact world
ladder.what_they_get: Your states, scoring tuned to your underwriting, integration into your CRM / dialer (Salesforce, GHL, …), your compliance vertical.

ladder.name: Build my whole data org
ladder.what_they_get: We become your data-infrastructure engine — this system, the next one, the next.

organvm/public-record-data-scrapper.cta_recruiter: Work with the team that built this
organvm/public-record-data-scrapper.seo_description: 50-state UCC public-records intelligence — fresh, enriched, scored MCA / financing leads via dashboard, REST API, and CLI.

organvm/universal-mail--automation.what_it_is: A production system that sweeps Gmail and Apple Mail across accounts, classifies every thread against a protocol ladder, tracks every owed reply in an obligations ledger, and composes voice-matched draft replies — draft-only, it never sends. Deploys as a Cloudflare Worker; runs keyless (IMAP app-password / Apple Mail osascript).
proof_signals: draft-only by design — composes replies, never sends
obligations ledger — every owed reply tracked
organvm/universal-mail--automation.buyer: Founders, operators, and the people who run their comms — anyone drowning in multi-account email who will not (and should not) hand an AI the send button.
organvm/universal-mail--automation.expensive_problem: Obligations fall through the cracks — the escalation you missed, the reply you owed a week ago — while triage burns the most expensive hours of the day. Every 'AI email tool' wants send authority nobody sane will give it.
organvm/universal-mail--automation.cost_of_not_having_it: Missed and late replies quietly cost deals and relationships, and the hours spent sorting an inbox by hand are the ones you can least afford to spend that way.
organvm/universal-mail--automation.public_face: the full system — source, the 400+ tests, the protocol ladder, the safety verifiers, how every piece is built. Read all of it.
organvm/universal-mail--automation.private_operation: the running instance tuned to your accounts, your protocol rules, and your voice — the live sweep, the ledger, the drafts kept current. The code is open; the engine that quietly runs your inbox (and never once sends without you) is the service.

ladder.name: Run my inbox triage
ladder.what_they_get: We run the sweep against your accounts and hand you a triaged inbox plus ready-to-send drafts for everything you owe a reply. You press send; we do everything up to it.

ladder.name: Deploy it on my infra
ladder.what_they_get: The system under your brand, on your infrastructure (or our managed Worker). Your team owns the triage pipeline instead of renting another SaaS.

ladder.name: Build it for my stack
ladder.what_they_get: Your protocol rules, your voice, integration into your CRM / helpdesk / ticketing, your compliance vertical — built to your exact workflow.

ladder.name: Run my comms ops
ladder.what_they_get: We become the engine behind your communications operation — this system, the next integration, the next surface.

organvm/universal-mail--automation.cta_recruiter: Work with the team that built this
organvm/universal-mail--automation.seo_description: Autonomous inbox triage for Gmail + Apple Mail — classifies threads, tracks every owed reply, drafts voice-matched responses. Draft-only, never sends.

organvm/a-i-chat--exporter.what_it_is: A browser userscript that exports your ChatGPT conversations into five portable formats — Markdown, HTML, JSON, PNG, and plain text — entirely in-browser. No account, no server, no data leaves your machine; it authenticates through your existing ChatGPT session and runs on chatgpt.com, chat.openai.com, and new.oaifree.com.
proof_signals: distributed on GreasyFork, GitHub, Docker (GHCR), and Cloudflare Pages
organvm/a-i-chat--exporter.buyer: Researchers, writers, developers, and knowledge workers who live in ChatGPT and need their conversations out — into Obsidian or Notion, a RAG pipeline, version control, or a compliance archive.
organvm/a-i-chat--exporter.expensive_problem: ChatGPT conversations are ephemeral by design. There's no native export to Markdown or HTML and no bulk download — the official dump is one unwieldy JSON blob most people can't use. Knowledge you paid for in thinking time evaporates or gets locked in a format you can't pipe anywhere.
organvm/a-i-chat--exporter.cost_of_not_having_it: Every lost thread is research you have to redo, context you can't feed your tools, and an archive you don't have the day a client, a court, or a coauthor asks for it.
organvm/a-i-chat--exporter.public_face: the entire userscript — every exporter, all 170 tests, the build, the localization. MIT-licensed; fork it, read it, ship it.
organvm/a-i-chat--exporter.private_operation: the maintained Pro build and the keyed bulk pipeline, kept working as ChatGPT's DOM shifts under it — plus the export engine built into your own product or workflow. The script is open; what you pay for is that it keeps working, at scale, the morning OpenAI changes everything.

ladder.name: Go Pro
ladder.what_they_get: Bulk export, multi-conversation, and the premium formats — the product, today, no call required.
ladder.internal_anchor: self-serve Pro via Lemon Squeezy (the product's own rail — see exporter pricing)

ladder.name: Run it for my team
ladder.what_they_get: We run bulk export across your team's accounts and hand back a clean, organized archive on a recurring basis — your formats, your destinations (Obsidian, Notion, a data lake).

ladder.name: Build it into my product
ladder.what_they_get: The export engine embedded in your app — your providers (Claude, Gemini), your formats, your branding. The hard part — surviving DOM churn across providers — already solved.

ladder.name: Run my data-portability layer
ladder.what_they_get: We own the moving-data-out problem across every AI tool your org uses — this exporter, the next provider, the next format — so your knowledge is never trapped.

organvm/a-i-chat--exporter.seo_description: Export ChatGPT conversations to Markdown, HTML, JSON, PNG, or text — in-browser, no account, no server. Bulk export and your own provider integrations.

organvm/mirror-mirror._publish_note: VERIFIED 2026-06-25 (HIGH confidence). organvm/mirror-mirror is currently PRIVATE — flip it public to render this onto the inbound surface (his hand: publishing source is an outward-facing identity change). Proof: ~42,800 lines TS, 152 source files, 60 feature components across a 40-tab app, 158 test files, Stripe + Lemon Squeezy billing, live on Netlify, Docker + Vercel + Netlify + Pages deploy.
organvm/mirror-mirror.display_name: Virtual Try-On Platform for Beauty & Salons
organvm/mirror-mirror.what_it_is: A 'phygital' beauty platform: hyper-realistic AR/3D virtual try-on, medical-grade skin diagnostics, AI style coaching, and salon booking in one app. Two modes — clients try on looks and book; stylists run a dashboard, manage consultations and proposals, and coordinate their team.
organvm/mirror-mirror.buyer: Beauty brands, salon groups, and cosmetics companies who want virtual try-on and consultation inside their own customer experience — and the consumers and stylists who use it.
organvm/mirror-mirror.expensive_problem: A salon visit costs hours and real money, and a wrong cut or color carries a six-to-eight-week regret cycle — so customers hesitate and bookings stall. A brand with no try-on misses the digital-first shopper entirely.
organvm/mirror-mirror.cost_of_not_having_it: Every hesitant customer is a booking that doesn't happen and a consultation that drags; for a brand, it's the engagement and conversion a try-on experience would have captured — going to a competitor who has one.
organvm/mirror-mirror.public_face: the full application — the try-on engine, the diagnostics, the 158 test suites, the billing integrations, every one of the 60 feature components. The whole build is readable.
organvm/mirror-mirror.private_operation: the running, branded instance — your catalog, your salons, your booking flow, the try-on engine licensed into your app and kept current. The code is open; the live experience your customers actually touch is the product.

ladder.name: Use the platform
ladder.what_they_get: Clients try on looks and book; the subscription tier unlocks the premium try-on and diagnostics.

ladder.name: Run it for my salon
ladder.what_they_get: The stylist dashboard, consultations, proposals, and booking under your brand, for your team.

ladder.name: Build try-on into my product
ladder.what_they_get: The try-on engine licensed and embedded in your own app — your catalog, your AR filters, your branding.

ladder.name: Run my virtual-try-on org
ladder.what_they_get: We become the team behind your try-on and beauty-tech surface — this platform, the next integration, the next brand.

organvm/mirror-mirror.seo_description: Virtual try-on for beauty and salons — AR/3D try-on, medical-grade skin diagnostics, AI coaching, and booking. White-label it or embed the engine in your app.

organvm/the-invisible-ledger._publish_note: VERIFIED 2026-06-25 (HIGH confidence). organvm/the-invisible-ledger is currently PRIVATE — flip it public to render (his hand). Proof: ~76,000 lines TS, 207 source files, 115 React components, 13 test suites (Vitest), Stripe checkout verified in code + tests, live on Netlify, Docker + GHCR + CI/CD, SHA-256 hash-chained audit log.
organvm/the-invisible-ledger.display_name: Chain-of-Custody Operations Platform for Privacy-First Firms
organvm/the-invisible-ledger.what_it_is: An operations dashboard for tax, wealth, and family-office firms whose clients are deliberately analog — air-gapped, courier-based, no cloud. It tracks every physical document from intake to vault with cryptographically-hashed custody events, routes by client psychographics, visualizes money flow, and runs anomaly detection and incident drills — so staff get mission-control visibility without ever putting a privacy-first client in the cloud.
organvm/the-invisible-ledger.buyer: Boutique tax & accounting firms, family offices, and wealth/estate practices serving privacy-conscious, high-net-worth clients who refuse cloud storage.
organvm/the-invisible-ledger.expensive_problem: When clients are air-gapped, custody lives in couriers, USB drives, and filing cabinets — and a single 'who touched this?' you can't answer, or one mishandled return, costs a relationship worth far more than any tooling. Off-the-shelf tools assume digital-native clients and solve none of it.
organvm/the-invisible-ledger.cost_of_not_having_it: One unprovable custody chain or mishandled document can end a high-net-worth relationship; the hours staff spend tracking paper by hand are the expensive ones, and the regulatory exposure has no ceiling.
organvm/the-invisible-ledger.public_face: the full platform — the custody tracker, the psychographic routing, the money-flow visualization, all 115 components and 13 test suites, the Stripe billing. Read the whole thing.
organvm/the-invisible-ledger.private_operation: the running instance keyed to your firm — your offices, your clients, your compliance vertical, the hash-chained ledger kept current and your staff's mission control live. The code is open; the operating system of record for your custody is the service.

ladder.name: Run it for my firm
ladder.what_they_get: The platform hosted and managed for your team — custody tracking, dashboards, compliance reports — per seat.

ladder.name: Run it on my infra
ladder.what_they_get: The platform under your brand, on your infrastructure — you own the custody pipeline instead of trusting a vendor's cloud.

ladder.name: Build it for my vertical
ladder.what_they_get: Your compliance regime, your client psychographics, integration into your document and practice-management stack.

ladder.name: Run my operations-tech org
ladder.what_they_get: We become the engine behind your firm's operational software — this platform, the next compliance surface, the next integration.

organvm/the-invisible-ledger.seo_description: Chain-of-custody operations platform for privacy-first tax, wealth, and family-office firms — hashed custody events, money-flow visualization, compliance reporting.

organvm/domus-genoma._publish_note: VERIFIED 2026-06-25 (HIGH confidence). organvm/domus-genoma is currently PRIVATE — flip it public to render (his hand). Proof: 8,106 lines (shell/Python/TS), 32 test files (BATS + pytest), 13 CI jobs, chezmoi provisioning + self-healing daemons + 1Password injection, tier catalog + Stripe infra (checkout endpoint pending). No public hosted demo — positioned honestly as a running framework, not a hosted SaaS.
organvm/domus-genoma.what_it_is: A productized provisioning platform built on chezmoi: declarative, templated configs that stand up an entire development environment across machines, with 1Password secret injection, self-healing daemons that detect and repair config drift, and a unified CLI. Three-tier licensing (personal, multi-machine, team) gated by JWT entitlements.
proof_signals: 1Password secret injection · zero secrets in Git
organvm/domus-genoma.buyer: Engineering and platform teams running more than one machine who are bleeding hours to environment setup, fighting config drift, and risking secrets leaking into Git.
organvm/domus-genoma.expensive_problem: Standing up a new machine takes hours, configs silently drift between devices until something breaks, and secrets slip into Git history — and when it breaks there's no clean way back. Multiply that across a team and it's a standing tax on every engineer.
organvm/domus-genoma.cost_of_not_having_it: Hours per machine per engineer, the security liability of secrets in repos, and the downtime when a divergent environment finally breaks mid-deploy — recurring, and entirely avoidable.
organvm/domus-genoma.public_face: the entire framework — the chezmoi templates, the self-healing daemons, the CLI, all 32 test files, the 13 CI gates, the licensing server. Clone it and run it.
organvm/domus-genoma.private_operation: the managed, fleet-wide operation — cloud-synced config across every machine in your org, drift caught and healed automatically, secrets injected from your vault, kept running. The framework is open; standing it up and keeping your whole fleet self-healing is the service.

ladder.name: Run it on my machines
ladder.what_they_get: The framework, free for a single machine; the paid tiers add cloud-sync and multi-machine.
ladder.internal_anchor: Personal free · Pro / Team tiers ($8 / $20 / mo, self-serve)

ladder.name: Provision my team
ladder.what_they_get: We stand up and manage cloud-synced, self-healing dev environments across your whole team's machines.

ladder.name: Build my internal developer platform
ladder.what_they_get: Your stack, your secrets backend, your compliance constraints — provisioning and self-healing built to your org's exact shape.

ladder.name: Run my platform-engineering org
ladder.what_they_get: We become your platform-engineering team — dev environments, fleet health, the next internal tool.

organvm/domus-genoma.cta_recruiter: Hire the platform engineer who built this
organvm/domus-genoma.seo_description: Declarative dev-environment provisioning on chezmoi — templated configs, 1Password secret injection, self-healing drift repair, cloud-sync across machines.

organvm/portfolio.what_it_is: A public portfolio site showcasing 16 completed production systems — each with a case study, real architecture diagrams (p5.js interactive), live demos where applicable, and the decision trees behind the build. Built with Astro, deployed on Netlify, fully indexed and discoverable.
proof_signals: 609 files · Astro + TypeScript · p5.js interactive visualizations
69 test files across the portfolio + content
16 case studies with architecture, decisions, and outcomes
organvm/portfolio.buyer: Prospects who want to see the builder's decision-making process, architects and design teams evaluating a platform partner, companies studying real production systems.
organvm/portfolio.expensive_problem: A portfolio of demos and one-liners doesn't prove judgment. Seeing the tradeoffs, the decisions, the outcomes, and the architecture is the difference between 'built something' and 'ships production systems'.
organvm/portfolio.cost_of_not_having_it: Vague feature lists and cherry-picked metrics. Real diligence requires seeing how a builder thinks through constraints, scales, and failure modes.
organvm/portfolio.public_face: All 16 case studies, the architecture diagrams, the interactive demos, the decision trees, the deployed live systems.
organvm/portfolio.private_operation: The internal evaluation — which projects to feature, what to emphasize, how the portfolio evolves as new systems are built.

ladder.name: Learn from the portfolio
ladder.what_they_get: Public access to the 16 case studies: architecture, decisions, scaled-to-production proof.

ladder.name: Deep-dive workshop
ladder.what_they_get: Interactive walkthrough of decision trees across 3–4 case studies; apply the thinking to your constraints.

ladder.name: Hire for a production pilot
ladder.what_they_get: Dedicate the builder to your constraint set. Design one system to your exact shape, deploy it, handoff the code and runbook.

ladder.name: Retain as platform director
ladder.what_they_get: Standing relationship: new systems, architecture reviews, on-call for scaling decisions, systems thinking as your company grows.

organvm/portfolio.seo_description: Production portfolio: 16 systems, real architecture decisions, interactive visualizations, case studies on building software at scale.

organvm/limen.display_name: Universal Agent Task Intake & Dispatch System
organvm/limen.what_it_is: A cross-agent, cross-repo task intake system. One YAML file (`tasks.yaml`) lives in every repo; every agent reads it, applies filters, and processes work. Includes a CLI for local task management, a REST API, a SaaS dashboard, and a CLI-driven task broker. Scales from single-agent to fleet-wide orchestration.
proof_signals: 35,811 lines of Python · 4,319 lines of TypeScript/JavaScript
54 pytest test files · 5 jest test files (59 total, 335+ passing)
MCP-accessible — exposes the task system over the Model Context Protocol (server + ianva doorway/aggregator)
Self-* organ ladder — self-sustaining, self-routing, self-feeding, self-healing, all live
organvm/limen.buyer: AI engineering teams running multiple agents across multiple repos; organizations scaling from single-agent to fleet orchestration.
organvm/limen.expensive_problem: Managing task routing across agents and repos is ad hoc — each agent has its own intake, routing is hardcoded, scaling to 5+ agents becomes coordination hell, and visibility into what's being worked is absent.
organvm/limen.cost_of_not_having_it: Duplicate work, missed priority signals, agents stepping on each other, no unified visibility into the pipeline, human wrangling of task assignments.
organvm/limen.public_face: The entire task system — CLI, API, the schema, the dashboard code, all 59 test files, the two deploy workflows, the multi-repo orchestration logic.
organvm/limen.private_operation: The running SaaS instance — your tasks, your agent runs, your historical logs, the live API that your fleet reads each beat.

ladder.name: Use it in your repo
ladder.what_they_get: Clone the tasks.yaml schema, run agents locally with limen CLI pointing at your repo.

ladder.name: Multi-repo orchestration
ladder.what_they_get: Run the dashboard and API on your infrastructure; orchestrate agents across your repo portfolio.

ladder.name: Cloud-hosted SaaS
ladder.what_they_get: Hosted limen dashboard, REST API, agents auto-scaled by Limen, historical logs and analytics.

ladder.name: Custom fleet orchestration
ladder.what_they_get: Limen running your entire agent fleet, tuned to your dispatch priority rules, on-call monitoring, capacity planning as you scale.

organvm/limen.seo_description: MCP-accessible multi-agent task orchestration: cross-repo task intake, universal dispatch schema, self-healing fleet-scale coordination over the Model Context Protocol.
